title: NASA and Boeing still uncertain about when Starliner will return to flight
summary: NASA and Boeing are uncertain about the return-to-flight timeline for the CST-100 Starliner, with potential delays of up to a year despite ongoing progress in addressing issues.

title: Trump executive order directs NASA to plan quantum space applications
summary: The White House issued an executive order to accelerate U.S. quantum technology development, including space applications for navigation, sensing, and secure communications. NASA has been directed to plan for these quantum space applications.

title: Rocket Lab launches satellite for U.S. Space Force Victus Haze responsive space exercise
summary: Rocket Lab launched a satellite for the U.S. Space Force as part of the Victus Haze responsive space exercise. The mission involved a Rocket Lab spacecraft paired with a True Anomaly vehicle to demonstrate rapid threat characterization and rendezvous operations.

title: Chinese spaceplane releases object into orbit, according to commercial space surveillance
summary: China's secretive spaceplane has released an object into orbit during its fourth mission, as reported by commercial space surveillance firm LeoLabs. The purpose of the object remains undisclosed.

title: America is about to cede Africa’s space industry to China, and nobody’s talking about it.
summary: The article warns that the U.S. may lose influence in Africa's space industry to China due to lack of engagement. It suggests that the Trump administration should prioritize sectors where the U.S. still holds an advantage.

title: Astroscale raises funding to support growth strategy
summary: Astroscale, a satellite servicing company, has secured new funding to shift from technology demonstrations to regular missions. The capital infusion aims to support its growth strategy.

title: Astrobotic says sale to Voyager will allow it to scale up
summary: Astrobotic will sell to Voyager Technologies to accelerate scaling for NASA's lunar base initiative. The acquisition aims to meet projected demands for lunar lander development.

title: India’s Jio lays out sovereign LEO constellation plan ahead of IPO
summary: Jio Platforms, India's largest telecom company, plans to launch a sovereign LEO satellite constellation to provide broadband services, aiming to lease capacity from existing constellations initially. This initiative is part of its strategy ahead of a potential IPO.

title: MDA Space to buy Blue Canyon Technologies to gain foothold in U.S. market
summary: MDA Space is acquiring smallsat manufacturer Blue Canyon Technologies for $620 million to strengthen its presence in the U.S. government market. The deal aims to leverage BCT's expertise and U.S. market access.

title: ElevationSpace Secures US $40 Million in Series B Funding, Bringing Total Raised to US $63.5 Million
summary: ElevationSpace Inc. raised an additional $40 million in Series B funding, bringing its total funding to $63.5 million. The company focuses on space-to-Earth transportation and space environment utilization and recovery platforms.

title: SpaceX launching its 1st 'Starfall' reentry capsule early on June 23: Watch it live
summary: SpaceX is set to launch its first 'Starfall' reentry capsule on June 23, marking a significant milestone in its spacecraft development. The mission aims to demonstrate the vehicle's capability to operate in space and return safely to Earth.

title: This ball of stars named Terzan 5 may be one of the Milky Way's original building blocks
summary: Astronomers have identified Terzan 5, a globular cluster, as a potential original building block of the Milky Way due to its unusual properties. The team's findings suggest it may hold clues about the galaxy's early formation.

title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope arrives in Florida ahead of SpaceX Falcon Heavy launch this summer
summary: NASA's Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for final preparations ahead of its SpaceX Falcon Heavy launch. The telescope will join other major space observatories like Hubble and Webb.

title: 'Project Hail Mary' is streaming now on MGM+, and you can watch for just $0.99 for a limited time
summary: The space-themed movie 'Project Hail Mary' is now available on MGM+ for a limited-time price of $0.99. The film follows Ryland and Rocky on an epic interstellar journey to save the galaxy.

title: Dark energy is still accelerating the expansion of the universe, and astronomers are relieved. 'Thankfully, we have averted this crisis'
summary: New research confirms that dark energy continues to accelerate the universe's expansion, dispelling recent claims of a cosmological crisis. Astronomers express relief as the standard model of cosmology remains intact.

title: 'Let's not fool the public': Why moon art should be more realistic in the Artemis age
summary: Experts urge for more realistic portrayals of the moon in art as NASA's Artemis program advances. Misrepresentations could mislead the public about lunar conditions.

title: Our expert thinks this pocket-friendly drone is brilliant for beginners, and it's $30 off ahead of Amazon Prime Day
summary: A beginner-friendly drone, DJI Neo, is highlighted as a great deal with a 15% discount before Amazon Prime Day. The drone is praised for its ease of use and affordability.

title: James Webb Space Telescope captures the star-forming clouds of Orion A in stunning detail | Space photo of the day for June 22, 2026
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has captured a stunning, colorful image of the star-forming clouds in the Orion A region. This detailed photo was featured as Space Photo of the Day on June 22, 2026.

title: SpaceX to test upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX is scheduled to launch a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle on Tuesday. This test aims to validate the vehicle's reentry capabilities.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This follows a busy month of activities aboard the station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space has transported the second stage of its Terran R heavy-lift rocket to the Stennis Space Center, marking progress toward its planned 2026 debut. The Terran R is a fully reusable launch vehicle under development by the company.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider faced weeks of postponements before scrubbing the mission.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. Flight 12 was completed about three weeks ago.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, marking a significant milestone in its space program. LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification of the first stage of its Nova rocket, marking a significant milestone in structural verification. This achievement brings the company closer to its goal of developing a fully reusable rocket.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission, which will test lunar landers in low Earth orbit. This crewed mission is part of the agency's efforts to return humans to the Moon.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned, but no specific details about the space industry news are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025, focusing on industrial opportunities and key priorities. These events aim to engage industry stakeholders and provide updates on ESA's planned activities.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A new advanced robotic arm is being developed for space applications, aiming to standardize robotic operations in orbit. This innovation could significantly enhance satellite servicing and maintenance capabilities.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space for its missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas to ensure mission success.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute deployment test, likely for a Mars or planetary landing, was conducted. The test involved baking or thermal conditioning of the parachute to ensure its reliability in extreme conditions.

title: A Faster Path to Orbit: How Hosted Payloads Are Changing Access to Space
summary: Hosted payloads are transforming the space industry by reducing time, cost, and complexity for deploying new capabilities in orbit. Companies now have a faster and more economical alternative to building everything in-house.

title: Exclusive: Northwood Unveils New Antenna, Expanded Network Capacity
summary: Northwood has unveiled a new antenna to expand its network capacity, supporting more data downlinks and a wider range of frequencies. This development is part of the company's rapid global expansion of its ground station footprint.

title: ElevationSpace Closes $40M Series B
summary: ElevationSpace, a Japanese satellite reentry startup, secured a $40M Series B funding round, bringing its total raised to $63.5M.

title: Boeing Completes Quantum Lab Test Ahead of 2027 Flight
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated quantum entanglement swapping in ground tests for its Q4S quantum networking satellite, aiming for a 2027 flight. The company is advancing quantum communication technology ahead of the planned mission.

title: True Anomaly Declares Mission X-3 Success
summary: True Anomaly announced the successful completion of Mission X-3 with its autonomous orbital vehicle, Jackal, achieving all test objectives and being fully commissioned.

title: Instinct Space Unveils Plans for Low-Cost Lunar Landers
summary: Instinct Space has announced a shift in focus from navigation support for lunar missions to developing low-cost lunar landers. The company aims to directly contribute to lunar surface exploration efforts.

title: Katalyst Raises $12M to Extend Satellite Servicing to GEO
summary: Katalyst has raised $12 million to expand its satellite servicing capabilities to geostationary orbit (GEO). The company plans to launch a mission closer to Earth this month, followed by its first GEO mission in 2027.

title: Dawn Aerospace Raises $25M Series B
summary: Dawn Aerospace has raised $25 million in a Series B funding round to accelerate the development of its Aurora spaceplane and conduct an in-space refueling demonstration.

title: Bill Spotlight: NEW HORIZON Act
summary: A bipartisan bill called the NEW HORIZON Act was introduced to allow national security missions to utilize commercial orbital data centers. The legislation aims to leverage the growing trend of commercial space-based data infrastructure.

title: Optera Raises £3M to Open New UK HQ
summary: Optera raised £3 million to establish a new UK headquarters, aligning with the UK government's push to enhance domestic technical capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Launches Responsive Victus Haze Mission for Space Force
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched the Tactically Responsive Space (TacRS) Victus Haze mission for the U.S. Space Force from New Zealand. This responsive space mission highlights advancements in rapid deployment capabilities.

title: Building a Global Framework for D2D With Skylo’s Mindel De La Torre
summary: Skylo's Mindel De La Torre discusses the rapid deployment of direct-to-device (D2D) satellite connectivity in millions of phones, transitioning from a tech demonstration to a mainstream feature.

title: EchoStar Swaps SATS Ticker for ECHO to Emphasize Business Shift
summary: EchoStar is changing its stock ticker from 'SATS' to 'ECHO' to reflect its shift into a global connectivity business. The change took effect on June 24.

title: Jio Evaluates a Sovereign LEO Constellation for India
summary: Jio Platforms, India's largest telecom operator, is considering launching a sovereign Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) constellation to provide satellite broadband services. The company is exploring a 'dual approach' to enhance its satellite broadband capabilities.

title: MDA Space to Acquire Blue Canyon from Raytheon to Reach US Defense Market
summary: MDA Space is acquiring spacecraft manufacturer Blue Canyon Technologies from Raytheon to expand into the U.S. defense market. The acquisition will strengthen MDA Space's position in the space industry.

title: Former SES Exec John-Paul Hemingway to Lead Vast’s Satellite Business
summary: Former SES executive John-Paul Hemingway has joined Vast to lead its satellite manufacturing business as Senior Vice President of Satellite Business Development and Commercialization.

title: Quantum Space to Demonstrate Orbital Refueling Under Pentagon Contract
summary: Quantum Space will demonstrate orbital refueling using its Ranger spacecraft under a Pentagon contract awarded on June 18. The company aims to showcase in-space servicing capabilities for national security applications.

title: Iceye Data to Support Flood Monitoring in Norway
summary: The Norwegian Water Resources and Energy Directorate (NVE) has partnered with Iceye to use its satellite data for flood monitoring in Norway. This collaboration leverages advanced satellite capabilities to enhance disaster response and water resource management.

title: Vantor and Rheinmetall Plan Joint Venture for Sovereign Space in Germany
summary: Vantor and Rheinmetall have announced plans to form a joint venture to develop sovereign space capabilities in Germany and Europe. The collaboration was formalized through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU).

title: Private Equity Firm EQT to Acquire Rideshare Provider Exolaunch
summary: Private equity firm EQT is acquiring rideshare provider Exolaunch, marking EQT's first investment in the space sector. The acquisition was announced on June 18.
